quality.q: In one sentence, what is the difference between TCP and UDP?
quality.a: TCP is a connection-oriented protocol that ensures reliable, ordered delivery of data through error-checking and acknowledgments, whereas UDP is a connectionless protocol that prioritizes speed and efficiency by sending data without verifying if it was received.

quality.q: A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep are left?
quality.a: There are **9** sheep left.   The phrase "all but 9 die" means that 9 survived.
